body.title: Add log error/failure grep step to day-close audit
body.result: Step 18.7 added to day-close-audit: grep last-2-day logs, suppress via allowlist; allowlist seeded with 12 known-ok patterns, 0 unsuppressed hits
body.description:
Day-close audit currently misses runtime errors in Igor's logs. Add a step that greps Igor log files for 'error' and 'failed' (case-insensitive), deduplicates, and produces follow-on tickets for unknown failures.

Phase 1 (CC): Claude analyses the first run — categorise each hit as known-ok (whitelist candidate) or real issue needing a ticket. Build the whitelist from that first pass.

Phase 2 (Scraps): Compile the allowlist + pattern matching into a deterministic Scraps script so future runs don't need inference — only new/unrecognised patterns escalate to CC.

Log paths to scan: ~/.TheIgors/logs/*.log, Igor channel messages (ring_memory), any device logs reachable from the rack.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md (new step), devices/scraps/ (new script), possibly lab/claudecode/audit_runner.py
**Design rules:** compiled-inference pattern — rules-first, LLM only for novel patterns
**Scope boundary:** grep + categorise + ticket generation. Not a real-time monitor — day-close batch only.
**Test plan:** run audit step against current logs, verify known-ok entries are suppressed, verify novel errors produce draft tickets
